title: Koeleria avenacea
taxonomy: Koeleria avenacea is classified as a species within the genus Koeleria, which belongs to the family Poaceae. This classification places Koeleria avenacea in the kingdom Plantae, and further within the division Magnoliophyta. It also falls under the class Liliopsida and the order Poales. Within its family Poaceae, Koeleria avenacea is specifically assigned to the subfamily Pooideae. The combination of these classifications allows for the precise identification of Koeleria avenacea within the plant kingdom.

intro: Koeleria avenacea, also known as June grass or early June grass, is a perennial grass species native to North America, belonging to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Koeleria avenacea has been used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria avenacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Koeleria avenacea is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments for topical application.
